title: Maximilianea tinctoria
intro: Maximilianea tinctoria, also known as bloodroot or red puccoon, is a perennial plant native to North America, belonging to the family Gentianaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its health benefits, including its potential to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne due to its anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Maximilianea tinctoria include alkaloids like berberine, sanguinarine, and chelerythrine, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Maximilianea tinctoria is often prepared as a tincture or salve, and is sometimes used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds, as well as internally in small doses for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects.

constituents: Maximilianea tinctoria, also known as Achiote, is a plant native to Central and South America. The active constituents of Maximilianea tinctoria include carotenoids, specifically the pigment beta-carotene, which is responsible for its vibrant red to orange color.
